# Spike-and-decay Fees

> Several core mechanisms across Polaris use spike-and-decay pricing.

Canonical URL: https://polaris-finance.github.io/polaris-docs/design/spike-and-decay-fees
Markdown URL: https://polaris-finance.github.io/polaris-docs/design/spike-and-decay-fees.md
Section: Protocol Design

Documentation index: https://polaris-finance.github.io/polaris-docs/llms.txt
Full documentation bundle: https://polaris-finance.github.io/polaris-docs/llms-full.txt

---

Several core mechanisms across Polaris use spike-and-decay pricing, including bonding curve swaps, pAsset minting and redemptions, and pETH-to-POLAR conversions. In particular, the first two have the same design with different parameters, while pETH-to-POLAR conversions use a Dutch auction mechanism.

Each of these mechanisms responds to a different type of market activity, but they all adjust their pricing according to the same market forces. As demand for a particular action increases, so does its cost. As demand fades, that cost gradually decays until interacting with the protocol becomes attractive again.

This allows prices to continuously adapt as market conditions evolve, enabling Polaris to capture more value during periods of higher demand while naturally encouraging activity again as costs decline.

Once deployed, the mechanism operates entirely through immutable protocol mechanics. Unlike parameters such as the conversion rebate, spike-and-decay pricing is not adjusted through stewardship and responds exclusively to market activity. The minimum and maximum fee ranges will be finalized closer to launch.
